title: support copying to compressed tarballs
body:
Can we support writing compressed tarballs?

For example, this writes a large uncompressed tarball:
```
skopeo copy docker://registry.example.com/myrepo:latest  docker-archive:mycontainer.tar
```

It would be great to support `--dest-compress`, like
```
skopeo copy docker://registry.example.com/myrepo:latest  --dest-compress docker-archive:mycontainer.tar.gz
```

comments.body:
I think it would be better to have it output to stdout 

A quick hack

skopeo copy docker://alpine:latest  docker-archive:/dev/stdout | gzip mycontainer.tar.gz

But this does not work.

I think the best we can do for now is just

skopeo copy docker://registry.example.com/myrepo:latest  docker-archive:mycontainer.tar; gzip mycontainer.tar

But I don't see a reason to build any direct compression into skopeo.

Thanks Dan.

For context, the reason I opened this ticket is that OSBS stores compressed tarballs in Koji, https://github.com/containerbuildsystem/atomic-reactor/issues/191 . There's been some discussion about that feature, and possibly updating OSBS handle archiving differently. If OSBS does not compress and archive images like that, users must use skopeo to download and archive all images uncompressed.

Note `podman save` does have a `--compress` flag to save local images. For example:

```
podman save --compress -o BZ1234567-v1.tar.gz --format oci-archive registry.example.com/example:bz1234567-v1
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I recommend using `podman save` as mentioned above. Unless there is no other way, I prefer to not have transport-specific options for transport-generic commands.
